     The Numbers Business Report

Captain Phillips (2013)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $107,136,417Details
International Box Office $113,511,767
Worldwide Box Office $220,648,184
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $19,214,530
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $12,966,651
Total $32,181,181
Further financial details...

Synopsis

A multi-layered examination of the 2009 hijacking of the U.S. container ship Maersk Alabama by a crew of Somali pirates. The film focuses on the relationship between the Alabama’s commanding officer, Captain Richard Phillips, and his Somali counterpart, Muse. Set on an incontrovertible collision course off the coast of Somalia, both men will find themselves paying the human toll for economic forces outside of their control.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$25,718,314 (24.0% of total gross)
Legs:4.17 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:48.6% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$55,000,000 (worldwide box office is 4.0 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,020 opening theaters/3,143 max. theaters, 7.5 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $157,589,828

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: October 11th, 2013 (Wide) by Sony Pictures
October 11th, 2013 (IMAX) by Sony Pictures
International Releases: October 11th, 2013 (Wide) (Aruba)
October 11th, 2013 (Wide) (Trinidad)
October 11th, 2013 (Wide) (Curacao)
October 17th, 2013 (Wide) (Argentina)
October 17th, 2013 (Wide) (Israel)
Video Release: January 21st, 2014 by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment
February 10th, 2014 by Sony Pictures
MPA Rating: PG-13 for sustained intense sequences of menace, some violence with bloody images, and for substance use.
(Rating bulletin 2287, 8/28/2013)
Running Time: 133 minutes
Award nomination: 2014 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Award win: Writers Guild of America Best Adapted Screenplay Winner
Film format: IMAX: DMR
Plot point: Hijacking, Hostage, On a Boat, Pirates, Rescue, Special Ops
Subgenre: Survival Drama
Source:Based on Real Life Events
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Dramatization
Production/Financing Companies: Columbia Pictures, Scott Rudin Productions, Michael De Luca Productions, Trigger Street Productions
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English, Somali

Synopsis

A multi-layered examination of the 2009 hijacking of the U.S. container ship Maersk Alabama by a crew of Somali pirates. The film focuses on the relationship between the Alabama’s commanding officer, Captain Richard Phillips, and his Somali counterpart, Muse. Set on an incontrovertible collision course off the coast of Somalia, both men will find themselves paying the human toll for economic forces outside of their control.
